Risk Probability Comparison

Model & Dataset Details

Technical information about the dataset, models and performance comparison

📦
Cleveland Heart Disease Dataset
Source: Kaggle / UCI Machine Learning Repository. One of the most widely used datasets for heart disease prediction research.
Total Samples1,000
Features13 clinical
Positive cases596 (59.6%)
Negative cases404 (40.4%)
Train / Test split800 / 200
🔬
Training Configuration
Train/Test Split: 80/20 stratified
CV Method: 10-Fold Stratified KFold
Scaling: StandardScaler (LR & RNN)
Class Weights: balanced (RF & LR)
Evaluation: Accuracy + AUC-ROC
Feature Descriptions
FeatureDescriptionRangeType
ageAge in years29–77Continuous
sexSex (1=Male, 0=Female)0, 1Binary
cpChest pain type (0=typical angina … 3=asymptomatic)0–3Categorical
trestbpsResting blood pressure on admission (mmHg)94–200Continuous
cholSerum cholesterol (mg/dl)126–564Continuous
fbsFasting blood sugar >120 mg/dl0, 1Binary
restecgResting ECG results0–2Categorical
thalachMaximum heart rate achieved during exercise71–202Continuous
exangExercise-induced angina0, 1Binary
oldpeakST depression induced by exercise relative to rest0–6.2Continuous
slopeSlope of peak exercise ST segment0–2Categorical
caNumber of major vessels coloured by fluoroscopy0–3Ordinal
thalThalassemia type (1=normal, 2=fixed, 3=reversible)1–3Categorical
🌲
Random Forest
n_estimators: 200
max_depth: 8
min_samples_split: 5
min_samples_leaf: 2
class_weight: balanced
random_state: 42
Feature Scaling: Not required

Builds 200 decision trees on random feature subsets and averages their probability votes. Handles non-linearity well and provides feature importances via mean decrease in impurity.
📈
Logistic Regression
solver: lbfgs
max_iter: 2,000
C: 1.0 (L2 regularisation)
class_weight: balanced
random_state: 42
Feature Scaling: StandardScaler

Applies sigmoid function on a linear combination of standardised features. Highly interpretable — each coefficient directly shows a feature's directional effect.
🧠
Neural Network (RNN)
Architecture: 13→128→64→32→16→1
Activation: ReLU
Solver: Adam
Alpha: 0.001
Early Stopping: Yes
Feature Scaling: StandardScaler

Deep neural network with 4 hidden layers using sequential architecture. Captures complex non-linear interactions between clinical features.
🏆 Winner: Random Forest
Random Forest achieved the highest Accuracy (75.0%) and AUC-ROC (0.823) on the held-out test set, making it the recommended primary model.

AUC-ROC

10-Fold CV
73.6%
AUC-ROC measures a model's ability to distinguish between positive and negative cases. Values closer to 1.0 indicate better discrimination regardless of threshold. 10-Fold CV reflects real-world generalisation performance on unseen data.
Random Forest Feature Importance
Mean decrease in impurity across all 200 decision trees
Chest Pain Type (cp) is the strongest predictor at 17.9%, followed by ST Depression (oldpeak) at 11.3%. This aligns with clinical practice — chest pain characteristics and ST segment changes are primary indicators of coronary artery disease.
